verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 (transforming growth factor beta 1) is a secreted cytokine that is synthesized as a pre-pro-protein, processed in the ER/Golgi, and secreted into the extracellular space as a soluble or ECM-sequestered ligand. It does not possess a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any intrinsic outer-leaflet lipidation. TGFB1 acts as a diffusible paracrine/autocrine factor that binds to TGF-β receptors on cell surfaces via reversible non-covalent interaction. The protein body itself never reaches the outer leaflet via its own mechanism; it remains soluble or ECM-deposited. While it can engage surface receptors, this is transient equilibrium binding, not a stable surface association. pMHC presentation is not the primary biology. Therefore, TGFB1 is not surface-accessible as a drug target antigen.
